hook: Brand new vs resale landed: the data shows who is still blinking first

slides.sub: New sales hold firm while resale prints soften — the gap is widening and the data tells you where.

slides.lead: Resale caveats are lodging at discounts that weren't there 18 months ago.

slides.pre: Sentosa Cove Coral Island — latest resale PSF
slides.post: Feb 2025 print: 737 sqm at S$14.8M — vs S$23,780 psm peak in Sep 2022. Lowest since mid-2021.

items.body: Coral Island: S$14.8M (Feb 25) vs S$17.4M (Feb 23) — same street, same tenure, -15% in two years.
Faber Hills semi-Ds peaked at S$8.4M (Dec 24) but the Sep 25 print slipped to S$6.6M — a 21% retreat.
New launches hold developer pricing; resale sellers absorbing the discount to move — bid-ask gap is real.

slides.title: Resale landed clusters by recent PSF

slides.label: The real signal
slides.punch: Sellers who bought at 2022–23 peaks are now the ones blinking. Coral Island gave back 15% in 24 months. Faber Hills semi-Ds shed 21% from peak to Sep 25. Resale buyers have leverage — but only if they know the exact comparable.
slides.punch_bold: The data gap between asking and transacted is where the deal lives.

items.body: Springwood (D5 RCR terrace, FH): 3 caveats in H2 2025 alone — S$3.9M to S$6.2M. Active, price-sensitive buyers.
Faber Hills (D5 OCR semi-D, FH): 5 caveats in 2024 — velocity held but median slipped from S$7.6M to S$6.9M.
Coral Island (D4 CCR detached, 99-yr): only 1 caveat in 2025 — thin volume, wide bid-ask, longer days-on-market.

slides.pre: Looking at landed in D4 / D5 / D9?
slides.title: Let's map your
slides.sub: 30-min strategy call. No pitch — just the caveats, the comparables, and the angles you won't find on a portal.

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone weighing resale vs new landed this month, send them this before they make an offer.

caption:
📉 Resale landed is blinking — and the caveats prove it.

• Coral Island detached: S$14.8M in Feb 25 vs S$17.4M two years ago — same street, -15%.
• Faber Hills semi-Ds shed 21% from their Dec 24 peak by Sep 25. Volume held, prices didn't.
• New launches hold developer pricing. Resale sellers are the ones absorbing the discount to move.

If you're buying, the leverage is real — but only if you know which comparable to use. That's exactly what the 30-min call is for.

📅 Book a 30-min strategy call → povguy.sg/strategy-call
